description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen encased in a rectangular wooden cabinet. The front features a prominent, rounded display bordered by a dark frame, with a vertical panel on the right side housing metallic dials, switches, and a speaker grille. The wood grain finish gives the exterior a classic, mid-20th-century appearance, while the back panel is made of beige plastic or metal with ventilation slits, control knobs, and technical labeling. The sides are flat and finished in the same wood veneer, providing a sturdy, boxy profile. The television's overall design is utilitarian, with visible wear and surface scratches on the screen, suggesting age and frequent use. This type of television would commonly be found in urban homes, garages, or even as discarded electronics on city streets.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood grain cabinet, convex glass screen, and a side panel of metallic dials and speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, wood frame, and a vertical panel with dials and speaker grille on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the flat wood grain panel and the depth of the television's cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the beige back panel with ventilation slits, control knobs, and technical labeling.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the uninterrupted wood grain panel and the television's boxy profile.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark-colored, wood-framed bezel with a metallic vertical panel on the right
attribute_control_button_layout: vertical cluster on the front right panel, including dials and switches
